sections.paragraph:
ἐπιμαρτυρέω to affirm that someth. is true, bear witness, attest (s. μαρτυρέω; Pla., Crat. 397a; Lucian, Alex. 42; Plut., Lys. 445 [22, 9] al.; PLond 1692a, 19 [VI a.d.]; Cat. Cod. Astrol. IX/1 p. 182, 27f; Jos., Ant. 7, 349; Tat. 3:3 ‘endorse’; Ath., R. 77, 21) foll. by acc. and inf. 1 Pt 5:12.—New Docs 2, 85f. M-M. TW.

someth. someth. = something

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

foll. foll. = followed, following

acc. acc. = accusative

inf. inf. = infinitive

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 375
